summary.intent: Diagnose why new checkpoints weren't being indexed and their messages weren't appearing in the database for session c15bd67e-428b-4a17-a658-3952485c9375.
summary.outcome: Two bugs were fixed: (1) server.ts was doing a no-op `reset --hard HEAD` instead of fetching from remote first, and (2) indexer.ts was creating duplicate SessionLink rows and re-scanning transcripts every poll cycle instead of using contentHash to skip already-indexed content. The server needed to be rebuilt and restarted to pick up the fixes.

repo: The gossamer checkpoint worktree lives at /private/tmp/gossamer-checkpoints and tracks the branch `entire/checkpoints/v1`

Session events come from two sources: hooks (write to LogEvent directly) and JSONL indexer (reads from checkpoint worktree files); session c15bd67e existed in the Session table but not LogEvent because indexer hadn't picked it up yet
JSONL files live in ~/.claude/projects/ until a checkpoint is committed to the branch — the indexer cannot see them before that
Checkpoint files are stored under /private/tmp/gossamer-checkpoints/<prefix>/<id>/<n>/ with metadata.json and full.jsonl
The frontend Electron app lives at /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/app and is started with `npm run dev`

code.finding: `reset --hard HEAD` was a no-op because HEAD never moved — needed `git fetch origin entire/checkpoints/v1` followed by `reset --hard FETCH_HEAD` to actually pull new commits

`db.sessionLink.create` (not upsert) caused a new SessionLink row to be created on every 30s poll cycle for every already-indexed checkpoint; fixed by looking up existing link by (checkpointMetadataId, transcript) and using contentHash to decide whether to skip or re-index
Events without `uuid` or `messageId` in full.jsonl get re-inserted on every poll cycle because the dedup sets only skip events with those fields; the contentHash skip now prevents this

workflow: After editing TypeScript source files the compiled binary must be rebuilt (`npm run build`) and the server process restarted before changes take effect
Checkpoint directory stubs with no metadata.json are a symptom of the worktree being frozen at a stale commit — the directories exist in the tree but file contents aren't checked out

friction: The stale worktree problem caused checkpoint dirs to appear as empty stubs, making it look like data was missing rather than not fetched — hard to distinguish from other causes
No unique constraint on SessionLink made the duplicate-row bug invisible until explicitly queried
The running server was using the old compiled binary, so fixes had no effect until rebuild+restart — required manual intervention outside the code changes
open_items: Session c15bd67e will only appear in LogEvent once `entire` commits a checkpoint for it to the branch — no guarantee this has happened yet
Events lacking both `uuid` and `messageId` in full.jsonl are still potentially problematic if contentHash changes mid-session (the dedup logic won't catch duplicates for those event types)
No unique constraint on SessionLink (checkpointMetadataId, transcript) — the code-level fix works but a DB constraint would make this more robust
